title: Nova Lingua Type Expression
description: Structured AST for Nova Lingua type expressions. v0.1 covers the basics: variables, references into the commons, built-in primitives and constructors, universal quantification, function types, type-constructor application, tuples, records, and sum types. Deliberately deferred to v0.2+: kind annotations, higher-rank polymorphism, type classes / traits, linear/affine types, existential types, GADTs, type-level lambdas. This schema validates structural shape only — well-formedness constraints (vars bound by an enclosing forall, ctor in 'apply' being a type-constructor, unique sum tags, unique record field names, canonical-form key ordering) are enforced by a separate validator. v0.1 of the function-record schema does NOT yet require this AST form for 'signature.type' (it still accepts a surface-syntax string); switchover is planned for function-record schema v0.2.

type.description: Any type expression. Discriminated by the 'kind' field.

name.description: Type-variable name. Must be bound by an enclosing forall (lexical scoping, enforced by separate well-formedness check).

var.description: A type variable, e.g. 'a' in 'forall a. List a -> List a'.

target.description: Content-address of a type artifact in the commons.

ref.description: A reference to a content-addressed type in the commons. Used for user-defined types whose identity is the hash of their definition.

name.description: v0.1 built-in vocabulary. Atomic types (lowercase: bool, int, nat, float, string, bytes, unit, never), common type constructors (PascalCase: List, Maybe, Result, Map, Set), and the nominal sum type Json (added in v0.4 — spec/expressiveness.md phase 3; the subject of the parse_json/render_json builtins, nullary: it takes no type arguments). Closed enum. v0.2+ will likely fold these into 'ref' once the primitives have stable hashes in the commons.

builtin.description: A built-in primitive type (atomic) or built-in type constructor. Distinguished from 'ref' because v0.1 does not yet have commons entries for the standard types; v0.2+ may unify.

vars.description: Type variables introduced by this forall. Canonical form sorts them lexicographically since 'forall a b. T' and 'forall b a. T' are alpha-equivalent in System F.

forall.description: Universal quantification. v0.1 supports only rank-1: foralls may appear at the outermost position of a type but not nested inside function-argument positions. Higher-rank polymorphism deferred to v0.2+.

params.description: Parameter types in positional order. Order is semantic and is preserved in canonical form.

fn.description: A function type with explicit multi-arg form (uncurried). 'a -> b' is represented as {params: [a], result: b}. A zero-arg function is {params: [], result: T}; this is distinct from 'unit -> T' though semantically equivalent in many languages.

ctor.description: The type constructor being applied. Typically a 'builtin' (e.g. List), a 'ref' (a user-defined constructor in the commons), or a 'var' (a higher-kinded type variable). v0.1 does not statically check that ctor has an appropriate kind — that is a well-formedness pass.

apply.description: Type-constructor application, e.g. 'List a' is {ctor: builtin List, args: [var a]}.

elems.description: Element types in positional order. Order is semantic.

tuple.description: An anonymous product type with at least two elements. The empty tuple is the 'unit' builtin (not a 0-element tuple); a 1-element tuple is just the element type.

fields.description: Named fields. JSON Schema cannot easily express 'unique by .name'; uniqueness is checked by the validator. Canonical form sorts fields lexicographically by name.

record.description: A product type with named fields. Distinct from 'tuple' because fields are addressed by name, not position.

tag.description: Variant tag. PascalCase by convention so tags don't collide with type-variable names.

type.description: Payload type for this variant. Omit for nullary variants (e.g. None, True).

variants.description: Sum variants. JSON Schema cannot easily express 'unique by .tag'; uniqueness is checked by the validator. Canonical form sorts variants lexicographically by tag.

sum.description: A tagged sum / variant type. Standard ADT form: each variant has a unique tag and an optional payload type.
